book_claims.claim: Consuming only non-starchy vegetables on fasting days powerfully shuts down the insulin/IGF-1/mTOR pro-ageing pathway
book_claims.mechanism: Absence of animal protein, grains and fruit combined with very low calorie content (~500 cal/day) inhibits the insulin/IGF-1/mTOR pathway that modulates ageing and cancer development
book_claims.study_ref: Refs 46, 47 in book; author's own clinical studies

book_claims.claim: Vegetable fasting 2-3 times per week causes significant weight loss without calorie counting
book_claims.mechanism: Very low calorie density of non-starchy vegetables keeps intake below 500 cal/day, achieving ~20-23% weekly calorie restriction

book_claims.claim: Vegetable fasting activates longevity pathways via hormesis
book_claims.mechanism: Fasting acts as chronic mild stressor (hormetic agent) that activates cellular protective and anti-ageing pathways, increases stress resistance, elevates cortisol moderately, and enhances removal of damaged proteins
BDNF promotes growth and survival of nerve cells, strengthening brain function

supplementary_research.finding: Review of fasting and calorie restriction: periodic fasting promotes stress resistance, reduces oxidative damage, and reduces IGF-1/insulin/mTOR signaling. Consistent with the book's mechanistic claims about vegetable fasting days.
Intermittent fasting triggers adaptive cellular stress responses including increased BDNF, improved autophagy, and enhanced DNA repair. Supports the hormesis mechanism described in the book.

Long-term calorie restriction in humans reduces IGF-1 only when protein intake is also restricted. This supports the book's emphasis on excluding animal protein (not just reducing calories) on fasting days.
NEJM review: intermittent fasting improves insulin sensitivity, reduces blood pressure, and decreases markers of oxidative stress. Multiple pathways converge on increased healthspan.

culinary_pairings.source: Book allows 1 tbsp at lunch and dinner on fasting days; essential for fat-soluble nutrient absorption
Permitted dressing on fasting days alongside olive oil and vinegar
Acetic acid may further improve insulin sensitivity; permitted on fasting days
Zero-calorie flavor enhancement; rosemary, basil, parsley, mint
Lettuce, arugula, endive, chicory form the base of fasting-day meals
Low-calorie, versatile non-starchy vegetable for soups and grills on fasting days
Crunchy, aromatic, very low calorie; satisfying raw or braised

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: The extreme low caloric density is the mechanism: unrestricted eating still stays under ~500 kcal/day, achieving the mTOR-suppressing effect.
Provides satiety on fasting days. Feeds gut bacteria producing short-chain fatty acids even during calorie restriction.
High water content aids hydration on fasting days and contributes to satiety.
Important electrolyte during fasting. Helps maintain blood pressure and muscle function.

synergies.description: Olive oil used as dressing on fasting days; each tablespoon adds ~100 calories so limited to 2 per day
Used as dressing alongside olive oil on fasting day vegetables
